title: Seseli simplicifolium
intro: Seseli simplicifolium, also known as wild lovage, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and fever, due to its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Seseli simplicifolium include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Seseli simplicifolium is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and other respiratory issues.
